pi_items.source: GitHub commits showing braille_alphabets.py activity in GCompris source code

Official KDE Applications page (https://apps.kde.org/gcompris/) lists braille system under 'Other' category alongside 'learn to tell time, maze, music instruments, etc.' Also confirmed by Google Play store listing and GCompris KDE documentation
No authoritative source found documenting the exact minimum number of letters required
Custom scoring scheme defined in task verifier.py, not part of actual GCompris

privileged_info_summary: The braille_alphabets activity is a real GCompris activity that teaches users the Braille alphabet system using a 6-dot interactive cell. The verified braille dot numbering system is correct: left column dots 1, 2, 3 (top to bottom) and right column dots 4, 5, 6 (top to bottom), with 'a' being represented by dot 1 alone. However, the task's metadata incorrectly categorizes this activity under 'Discovery' when the official GCompris categorization places it in the 'other' category (which includes miscellaneous learning activities like telling time, mazes, and music instruments). The task's activity name 'braille_alphabets' is correct, but no authoritative source confirms the requirement for exactly 3 minimum letters to complete.
